content.problemStatement: Develop a function to compute and return the area of a rectangle, given its length and width.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space-separated integers L and W, the length and width of a rectangle.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the area of the rectangle (L × W).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Length 5 multiplied by width 8 gives area 40.
Length 5 multiplied by width 5 gives area 25.

solution.approach: The area of a rectangle is the product of its length and width. For each test case we read L and W and output L*W. This is O(1) per test case and uses only constant extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int L,W; scanf("%d %d",&L,&W); printf("%d\n",L*W);} return 0;}
code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        L = int(data[idx]); W = int(data[idx+1]); idx += 2
        out.append(str(L*W))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
